symptoms.symptom_name: Severe episodic poorly-localised visceral abdominal pain, unrelieved by OTC analgesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe neurovisceral abdominal pain out of proportion to examination is the commonest presenting feature of an acute attack.
Non-specific — severe abdominal pain is the anchoring trap toward a surgical cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: New bilateral proximal upper-limb weakness (acute motor neuropathy, patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute motor neuropathy in a porphyric attack can ascend to respiratory muscles — the immediate threat to life.
Shared feature; keeps GBS on the list as an important-not-to-miss cause of ascending weakness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urine darkening to reddish-brown on standing under light

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Porphobilinogen oxidises on light exposure, darkening the urine — a classic, patient-observable pointer to an acute porphyria.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recently completed a sulfonamide antibiotic (porphyrinogenic trigger)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sulfonamides are recognised porphyrinogenic drugs that induce ALA synthase and precipitate acute attacks; identifying the precipitant is pivotal.

symptoms.symptom_name: Autonomic surge reported — profuse diaphoresis and palpitations

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic autonomic instability is a core feature of the acute neurovisceral attack.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute confusion, paranoia and impending doom

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neuropsychiatric features (anxiety, confusion, psychosis) are part of the acute attack and, with the somatic findings, argue against a primary psychiatric cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vomiting with three days of absolute constipation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic gut involvement (ileus, vomiting, constipation) accompanies the visceral pain of an acute attack.
Absence of fever makes a primary septic/infective abdomen less likely as the driver of this presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft abdomen without rebound or guarding despite severe pain (pain-examination mismatch)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A soft, non-peritonitic abdomen despite excruciating pain argues strongly against a surgical abdomen and toward a neurovisceral metabolic cause — but this is a clinician examination finding, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Proximal power 4/5 with diminished biceps reflexes (objective neuro exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective motor deficit confirms the acute neuropathy; a sealed clinician finding — the AI Doctor infers weakness from the patient's report, not from a measured exam.
Objective autonomic instability; sealed clinician measurement — no home device is offered in this case, so the AI infers the autonomic surge from the reported sweating and palpitations.

investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated PBG and ALA during the acute attack
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The confirmatory test is missed; diagnosis remains unproven, but emergency treatment must not wait for it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Grossly elevated urinary PBG/ALA during an attack is diagnostic of an acute porphyria — a hospital laboratory test, never obtainable in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe visceral pain out of proportion + acute motor neuropathy + reddish-brown photosensitive urine + autonomic instability + acute neuropsychiatric change, following a porphyrinogenic drug, is a near-specific pattern for an acute porphyric attack.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urinary porphobilinogen (PBG) and ALA (sample shielded from light)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirmatory test for an acute porphyria; arranged through the ED, and the urine must be protected from light. Emergency treatment should not wait for the result.
Hyponatraemia from SIADH is common in an acute attack and can cause seizures; assessed in hospital.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine outpatient porphyria screen in place of emergency transfer
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Deferring to an outpatient work-up would be a dangerous under-triage — this is an emergency requiring immediate transfer, not a clinic test.
